title: Replace @Controller with @RestController in Spring applications
description: In Spring applications, the @RestController annotation is a specialized version of @Controller that combines @Controller and @ResponseBody. Using @RestController for RESTful web services simplifies code and avoids the need to annotate every method with @ResponseBody. This rule flags usages of @Controller where @RestController would be more appropriate.
recommendation: Use @RestController instead of @Controller for REST endpoints to ensure correct response serialization and reduce boilerplate.
impact: Using @Controller instead of @RestController for REST APIs may result in missing @ResponseBody annotations, leading to incorrect response handling.

target_check.message: Use @RestController instead of @Controller for REST endpoints to ensure correct response serialization and reduce boilerplate.
